qa_pairs.question: What is the name of the directory created by ACPYPE when processing 'atp.sdf'?
What types of files are generated by ACPYPE in its output directory?
qa_pairs.answer: ACPYPE generates various files in its output directory, including log files (acpype.log), Amber topology and coordinate files (e.g., atp_AC.prmtop, atp_AC.inpcrd), GROMACS topology and coordinate files (e.g., atp_GMX.top, atp_GMX.gro), CHARMM topology and parameter files (e.g., atp_CHARMM.rtf, atp_CHARMM.prm), CNS/XPLOR topology and parameter files (e.g., atp_CNS.top, atp_CNS.par), and various intermediate files and run parameters (e.g., em.mdp, md.mdp).

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find a list of Frequently Asked Questions about ACPYPE?
qa_pairs.answer: Frequent Asked Questions about ACPYPE can be found at https://github.com/alanwilter/acpype/wiki/Frequent-Asked-Questions-about-ACPYPE.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a tutorial available for using ACPYPE with GROMACS?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a tutorial for using ACPYPE with GROMACS is available at https://github.com/alanwilter/acpype/wiki/Tutorial-Using-ACPYPE-for-GROMACS.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one find a tutorial for using ACPYPE with NAMD?
qa_pairs.answer: A tutorial for NAMD is available at https://github.com/alanwilter/acpype/wiki/Tutorial-NAMD.
